SEARCH
Package javalibs

Class SysHelper

java.lang.Object
javalibs.SysHelper
public class SysHelper extends Object
Copyright 2019 Sean Grimes. All rights reserved. License: MIT License Most functionality is provided by https://github.com/oshi/oshi with some logic to handle situations where oshi output is not available. An important note: Much of the information here is slow to gather, relative to CPU time. Determining the number of physical processors, as a point of reference, is a 2-5 millisecond operation on my system. This could vary wildly based on OS and hardware. These are not cheap operations to perform - at least the first time. See below. A happy surprise: Oshi, happily, seems to memoize as many hardware / os related calls as possible. This is fantastic news. As an example the first call to osName takes 3500 microseconds on my machine while the second call takes 5 microseconds.

    Gets OS manufacturer depending on available information

    Get the operating system name

    Gets the OS version where available

    Get the specific build information for the OS.

    Attempts to determine the number of physical processor cores

    prettyUptime()
    Get a good looking representation of the total system uptime
    int
    reportedCPUCoreCount()
    Attemps to return the total number of logical CPUs available.
    String
    rootFreeSpace()
    Attempts to get the total free space available on the root partition NOTE: This is *NOT* robust and I only guarantee it to work on my machine
    String
    rootTotalSpace()
    Attempts to get the size of the root partition NOTE: This is *NOT* robust and I only guarantee it to work on my machine
    boolean
    runningOnAC()
    Returns true if verified AC power, else false.
    String

    Returns the total available memory *now* as reported by the Runtime Will return in GB / MB / KB or bytes based on the most appropriate unit

  • Method Details

    • get

      public static SysHelper get()

    • osName

      public String osName()
      Get the operating system name
      Returns:
      OS name, as String

    • osVersionFullInfo

      public String osVersionFullInfo()
      Get the specific build information for the OS. Will fallback to System.getProperty with "os.name" key if full os information is not available
      Returns:
      OS build information, as String

    • osBuildNumber

      public String osBuildNumber()
      Gets the OS build number where available
      Returns:
      The build number or an empty string

    • osCodeName

      public String osCodeName()
      Gets the OS code name where available
      Returns:
      The code name or an empty string

    • osManufacturer

      public String osManufacturer()
      Gets OS manufacturer depending on available information
      Returns:
      The os manufacturer or an empty string

    • osVer

      public String osVer()
      Gets the OS version where available
      Returns:
      The os version of an empty string

    • physicalCPUCoreCount

      public int physicalCPUCoreCount()
      Attempts to determine the number of physical processor cores
      Returns:
      The total physical cores if available, else an approximation

    • cpuMaxBaseFreq

      public long cpuMaxBaseFreq()
      Try to determine the max frequency of the CPU
      Returns:
      The max frequency, else 0

    • cpuTemp

      public double cpuTemp()
      Attempt to get the current CPU temperature in Celsius
      Returns:
      Return the CPU temp if available, else 0

    • reportedCPUCoreCount

      public int reportedCPUCoreCount()
      Attemps to return the total number of logical CPUs available.
      Returns:
      Number of logical processors available

    • hasHyperThreading

      public boolean hasHyperThreading()
      Attempts to determine if hyperthreading is enabled
      Returns:
      True if it seems like hyperthreading is enable, else false.

    • hostName

      public String hostName()
      Tries to determine the machine's hostname
      Returns:
      The machine's hostname if available else empty string

    • hardwareManufacturer

      public String hardwareManufacturer()
      Tries to determine the hardware manufacturer
      Returns:
      The manufacturer if available else emoty string

    • hardwareModel

      public String hardwareModel()
      Tries to determine the machine's model
      Returns:
      The model if available else empty string

    • hardwareSerial

      public String hardwareSerial()
      Tries to determine the hardware serial number
      Returns:
      The serial number if available else empty string

    • uptimeSeconds

      public long uptimeSeconds()
      Attempts to determine how long the machine has been up for
      Returns:
      Uptime in seconds if available, else -1

    • prettyUptime

      public String prettyUptime()
      Get a good looking representation of the total system uptime
      Returns:
      Uptime in the x day, x hour, x minute, x second format

    • elevated

      public boolean elevated()
      Determine if running as sudo / Administrator
      Returns:
      True if elevated permissions else false

    • runningOnAC

      public boolean runningOnAC()
      Returns true if verified AC power, else false. False doesn't guarantee battery power, it does guarantee that the function couldn't say for sure that the machine is running on AC power
      Returns:
      True if on A/C and verified, else false

    • batteryTimeRemainingSeconds

      public double batteryTimeRemainingSeconds()
      Returns an estimated runtime on battery as reported by the OS where available. If the system appears to be plugged in the result will be Double.MAX_VALUE. If information is unavailable the result will be -1.0.
      Returns:
      Estimated runtime on battery in seconds

    • batteryCycleCount

      public int batteryCycleCount()
      Try to determine the current cycle count on the battery, if available
      Returns:
      The current battery cycle count, else -1

    • userName

      public String userName()
      Get the user's username
      Returns:
      The username of the user

    • userHome

      public String userHome()
      Get the user's home directory
      Returns:
      The path of the user's home directory

    • userWorking

      public String userWorking()
      Get the current working directory
      Returns:
      Path of the current working directory

    • javaVer

      public String javaVer()
      Get the version of the running JVM
      Returns:
      JVM version

    • JVMVendor

      public String JVMVendor()
      Get the vendor of the JVM
      Returns:
      JVM vendor string

    • maxMem

      public String maxMem()
      Returns the max available memory as reported by the Runtime Will return in GB / MB / KB or bytes based on the most appropriate unit
      Returns:
      Runtime reported max memory

    • totalMem

      public String totalMem()
      Returns the total available memory *now* as reported by the Runtime Will return in GB / MB / KB or bytes based on the most appropriate unit
      Returns:
      Runtime reported total memory available *now*

    • freeMemory

      public String freeMemory()
      Returns the total free memeory *now* as reported by the Runtime Will return in GB / MB / KB or bytes based on the most appropriate unit
      Returns:
      Runtime reported free memory available *now*

    • rootTotalSpace

      public String rootTotalSpace()
      Attempts to get the size of the root partition NOTE: This is *NOT* robust and I only guarantee it to work on my machine
      Returns:
      The size of the root partition

    • rootFreeSpace

      public String rootFreeSpace()
      Attempts to get the total free space available on the root partition NOTE: This is *NOT* robust and I only guarantee it to work on my machine
      Returns:
      Free space on root partition
